channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: ‘Estoy manchado. Estoy en los archivos’
item.description: Atrás quedaron las cenas de lujo en mansiones de donantes y la ropa de diseñador. Ahora David Ross paga el precio de haberse relacionado con el abusador sexual Jeffrey Epstein.
item.dc:creator: Eli Saslow and Erin Schaff
item.category: San Francisco Museum of Modern Art

media:content.media:credit: Erin Schaff/The New York Times

item.title: Este músculo es el héroe anónimo de la longevidad
item.description: Fortalecer los glúteos te ayudará a mantenerte libre de lesiones y a ser independiente en tu adultez y vejez.
item.dc:creator: Hilary Achauer

media:content.media:credit: Nicholas Sansone for The New York Times

item.title: Out the Door
item.description: Tell us a story, real or made up, that is inspired by this image.
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Mamdani Promised to Freeze the Rent. Now the Fight Begins.
item.description: Nine board members, six of whom were appointed by Mayor Zohran Mamdani, will meet Thursday to begin weighing whether to enact a rent freeze.
item.dc:creator: Mihir Zaveri

media:content.media:credit: Kent J. Edwards for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Rent Guidelines Board will decide later this year whether to freeze the rent for the city’s nearly one million rent-stabilized apartments.

item.title: Why a 100-Foot Protest ‘Buffer Zone’ at Houses of Worship Won’t Happen
item.description: A bill being voted on Thursday is a watered-down version of what the New York City Council speaker vowed to pass after an intense protest outside a synagogue last year.
item.dc:creator: Dana Rubinstein and Sally Goldenberg

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The New York City Council speaker, Julie Menin, dramatically watered down a bill that would have allowed a buffer zone for protests outside of houses of worship.

item.title: Battle Over a Contentious Police Unit Re-emerges in New York City
item.description: A bill will be introduced on Thursday to restrict the Police Department’s Strategic Response Group from being deployed at protests.
item.dc:creator: Maia Coleman and Dana Rubinstein

media:content.media:credit: Victor J. Blue for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Strategic Response Group has been criticized for occasionally using military-style tactics to control mostly peaceful protesters.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for March 26, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Palabra del día en inglés: ‘exemplary’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 180 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Los restos de un misil que cayó en una escuela, en una foto
item.description: Los niños de una escuela de Cisjordania encontraron en el patio de su escuela un recuerdo de la guerra que viven a diario: un enorme fragmento de un misil iraní.
item.dc:creator: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times

item.title: Word of the Day: exemplary
item.description: This word has appeared in 180 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Josh Johnson Ridicules the Army’s Opening to Older Recruits
item.description: “Look, according to People magazine — and apparently the U.S. military — 42 is the new 35,” Johnson said on Tuesday’s “Daily Show.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: Para Putin, la guerra en Irán lo cambió todo
item.description: El ataque conjunto de Estados Unidos e Israel disparó los precios del petróleo y fracturó las relaciones dentro de la OTAN, lo que significó buenas noticias para Moscú.
item.dc:creator: Mikhail Zygar

media:content.media:credit: Foto de consorcio por Alexander Zemlianichenko

item.title: Ya no es la madrastra malvada: la reivindicación de una poderosa faraona
item.description: Una reevaluación de estatuas dañadas de 3500 años de antigüedad se suma a las pruebas de que la reina Hatshepsut no fue la villana que los estudiosos creían.
item.dc:creator: Franz Lidz

item.title: It Was One of the Cold War’s Greatest Crimes. No One Has Paid a Price.
item.description: No one apart from the Congolese people has ever paid a price for the CIA-backed assassination of Patrice Lumumba.
item.dc:creator: Stuart A. Reid

media:content.media:credit: Bettmann/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Patrice Lumumba in Léopoldville (today Kinshasa, Democratic Republic of Congo) after his arrest in December 1960.

item.title: Chinese Education Influencer’s Sudden Death Prompts Grief, and Reflection
item.description: The influencer, Zhang Xuefeng, was known for no-nonsense, some said cynical, advice about how to win in China’s educational rat race. He died at 41.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Wang

media:content.media:credit: Feature China/Future Publishing, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Zhang Xuefeng during a lecture at a university in the central city of Zhengzhou in 2017. He rose to fame for his advice on how to succeed in a hypercompetitive environment.

item.title: Rescuers Work to Save a 32-Foot Humpback Whale Trapped in the Baltic Sea
item.description: The whale had been stuck for days in shallow waters of the Baltic Sea, as rescuers prepared to make another attempt to free it.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross

media:content.media:credit: Sea Shepherd, via Agence France-Presse - Getty Image
media:content.media:description: A photograph released by Sea Shepherd Deutschland showing a stranded humpback whale off the Baltic Sea coast of Timmendorfer Strand near Lübeck, northern Germany, on Tuesday.

item.title: Limpieza de primavera: estos 7 mitos son tus peores enemigos
item.description: No hay que hacer todo de una vez, ni aferrarse a todas las pertenencias, ni lanzarse a comprar canastas y organizadores antes de empezar. Te decimos qué más recomiendan los expertos.
item.dc:creator: Dorie Chevlen

item.title: Trump’s Threats to Europe Put Its Leaders in a Double Bind Over Iran
item.description: European politicians risk angering their voters if they join America’s war. Yet they could also face domestic upheaval if they take no action to reopen shipping routes that Iran has blocked and ease an energy crisis.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump disembarking from Air Force One in Maryland on Monday.

item.title: New York City’s Population Flat After Drop in Immigration
item.description: Newly released census data shows that from June 2024 to July 2025, the number of new residents who were international immigrants dropped by 70 percent compared to a year before.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Hu and Jeff Adelson

media:content.media:credit: Todd Heisler/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Many large urban counties saw sharp declines in international migration, though none as substantial as in New York City.

item.title: Immigration Slowdown Hits Every Metro Area in the U.S., Census Shows
item.description: Large urban counties and the border were the most affected. And in three-quarters of U.S. counties, population growth either slowed or turned negative.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Adelson and Amy Qin

media:content.media:credit: IMAGO/Zoonar, via Reuters
media:content.media:description: The population of Los Angeles County, the country’s most populous, declined by nearly 54,000.

item.title: How U.S.A.I.D. Birth Control Meant for Africa Was Ruined
item.description: The Trump administration had options for offloading contraceptives once destined for Africa, a newly obtained memo shows. Instead, it has let them collect dust and go bad.
item.dc:creator: Jeanna Smialek and Stephanie Nolen

media:content.media:credit: Hilary Swift for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A warehouse in Geel, Belgium, where contraceptives have been stranded since U.S.A.I.D. was defunded and dismantled last year.

item.title: Corrections: March 26, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Thursday, March 26, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Have a Nice Flight. Don’t Forget Your Fortitude.
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Thursday, March 26, 2026.

item.title: Trump Calls for Law Cracking Down on Crime and ‘Rogue Judges’
item.description: Ahead of the midterms, President Trump told a Republican gathering that he wants to go after repeat offenders and “rogue judges that are criminals.”
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump and top aides have been railing against judges who have ruled against him, including justices on the Supreme Court.

item.title: Chip Taylor, Writer of ‘Wild Thing’ and Other Classic Rock Hits, Dies at 86
item.description: Mr. Taylor, the younger brother of the actor Jon Voight, found success as a songwriter for the likes of Janis Joplin and Juice Newton.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ziegler

media:content.media:credit: Ruth Fremson/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Chip Taylor in 2003. “I wasn’t sure about ‘Wild Thing’ at first,” Mr. Taylor recalled in an interview in 1999. “On the demo I told the sound engineer to turn off the lights in the studio, and I just sat on a stool and sang it like a blues guy would.”

item.title: Melania y el robot
item.description: La primera dama, Melania Trump, cree que más niños deberían contar con “educadores humanoides”.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rogers

media:content.media:credit: Oliver Contreras/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Melania Trump, la primera dama, con Figure 3, un robot humanoide desarrollado por Figure AI, una empresa de robótica de inteligencia artificial.

item.title: Muere uno de los creadores de OnlyFans, Leon Radvinsky
item.description: Radvinsky convirtió un diminuto sitio web llamado OnlyFans en una potencia del entretenimiento para adultos, redefiniendo el sector para la era de las redes sociales.
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for March 26, 2026
item.description: Is Alex Murphy practicing reverse psychology on us?
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

item.title: Schumer Pledges Democrats Will Restore Clean Energy Tax Credits
item.description: Senator Chuck Schumer of New York, the minority leader, said Democrats would pursue an agenda to reduce energy costs if they win back control of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Friedman

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Chuck Schumer, the Senate minority leader, at the Capitol this week.

item.title: Mamdani Now Plans $1.3 Billion in Cuts in Programs That He Favored
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani is banking on savings from a delay he is seeking in the state mandate for smaller class sizes. He is also scaling back a commitment to expand a rental assistance program.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Oreskes and Sally Goldenberg

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani faces a $5.7 billion budget deficit that he must fill by July 1.

item.title: Trump’s ‘Brute Force Imperialism’
item.description: From saber rattling over Greenland to war with Iran, the Opinion columnist Jamelle Bouie argues, treating the world like a game of Risk has left America in its weakest position since World War II.
item.dc:creator: Jamelle Bouie and Ingrid Holmquist
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Trump dice que está en conversaciones con Irán, pero Teherán lo niega. Explicamos la razón
item.description: El presidente Trump tiene motivaciones políticas nacionales e internacionales para pregonar que está en negociaciones para poner fin a la guerra. Irán tiene razones similares para negar las conversaciones.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: El presidente Trump ha suscitado esperanzas de que la guerra con Irán pueda terminar pronto.

item.title: ICE Agents at Some Airports Begin Checking IDs in Security Lines
item.description: It was unclear whether having agents helping with screening passengers would improve wait times. Some travelers expressed worries.
item.dc:creator: Christina Morales, Christine Chung, Hamed Aleaziz, Sean Keenan and Rowan Moore Gerety

media:content.media:credit: Megan Varner/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents at Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport in Atlanta on Wednesday.

item.title: The Idiot: Chapter 5
item.description: With Allen set to be released in a few years, M. wonders if he will be able to come back into the family fold.
item.dc:creator: M. Gessen, Daniel Guillemette, Andrey Borzenko and Lika Kremer of Libo/Libo Studios, Julie Snyder, Allison Leyton-Brown, Phoebe Wang and Catherine Anderson

item.title: The Idiot: Chapter 4
item.description: M. and Allen finally speak. M. develops a theory of the case. Or one of Allen, at least.
item.dc:creator: M. Gessen, Daniel Guillemette, Andrey Borzenko and Lika Kremer of Libo/Libo Studios, Julie Snyder, Allison Leyton-Brown, Phoebe Wang and Catherine Anderson

item.title: The Idiot: Chapter 3
item.description: M. attends Allen’s trial in San Francisco, where undercover recordings reveal how Allen’s plan to get Priscilla deported from the U.S. turned into a murder-for-hire plot.
item.dc:creator: M. Gessen, Daniel Guillemette, Andrey Borzenko and Lika Kremer of Libo/Libo Studios, Julie Snyder, Allison Leyton-Brown, Phoebe Wang and Catherine Anderson

item.title: The Idiot: Chapter 2
item.description: Allen’s ex-wife, Priscilla, tells M. about the lengths she has had to go to reunite with her son. M. is shocked.
item.dc:creator: M. Gessen, Daniel Guillemette, Andrey Borzenko and Lika Kremer of Libo/Libo Studios, Julie Snyder, Allison Leyton-Brown, Phoebe Wang and Catherine Anderson

item.title: The Idiot: Chapter 1
item.description: The New York Times writer M. Gessen always disliked their younger cousin Allen. But then he did something so bad, even M. was surprised – and they realized they didn’t know Allen at all.
item.dc:creator: M. Gessen, Daniel Guillemette, Andrey Borzenko and Lika Kremer of Libo/Libo Studios, Julie Snyder, Allison Leyton-Brown, Phoebe Wang and Catherine Anderson

item.title: N.Y.P.D. Inspector Accused of Groping an Officer Is Stripped of His Gun
item.description: After a policewoman in the Bronx complained that an inspector was sexually harassing and groping her, he remained her supervisor for more than a year, according to her lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Maria Cramer

media:content.media:credit: Hiroko Masuike/The New York Times

item.title: ‘Love Story’ Is Actually A Horror Story
item.description: Wesley Morris and Sasha Weiss, the culture editor of The New York Times Magazine, on FX’s streaming hit “Love Story: John F. Kennedy Jr. & Carolyn Bessette.”
item.dc:creator: Pat Gunther, Amy Marino, Alfredo Chiarappa, Daniele Sarti, Austin Mitchell and Felice Leon
item.category: Love Story: John F. Kennedy Jr. & Carolyn Bessette (TV Program)

item.title: Republicans in Congress Fret Over Trump Administration’s Handling of Iran War
item.description: G.O.P. lawmakers who have given the Trump administration wide latitude to wage war with no congressional input are growing frustrated as officials offer little detail about ground troops, cost or timeline.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison, Megan Mineiro and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: “We will not sacrifice American lives for the same failed foreign policies,” Representative Nancy Mace of South Carolina said on Wednesday.

item.title: Justice Dept. Settles Flynn’s Wrongful Prosecution Suit for $1.25 Million
item.description: The agreement was an extraordinary example of how the Trump administration has offered legal relief to those aligned with the president.
item.dc:creator: Alan Feuer
item.category: Russian Interference in 2016 US Elections and Ties to Trump Associates

media:content.media:credit: Tom Brenner for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Michael T. Flynn, seen in 2018, briefly served as President Trump’s national security adviser in his first term.

item.title: Judge Orders Arrest of Matt Bevin, Former Kentucky Governor, for Contempt
item.description: Seven years after leaving office, Matt Bevin, the combative ex-governor, faces charges for not providing financial records sought by his estranged son.
item.dc:creator: Campbell Robertson

media:content.media:credit: Luke Sharrett for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Matt Bevin at his election night party in Louisville, Ky., after losing his re-election bid in 2019.

item.title: Meta y YouTube son declarados negligentes en caso de adicción a las redes sociales
item.description: Un jurado determinó que las empresas perjudicaron a una joven usuaria al diseñar productos que le crearon adicción y le provocaron trastornos mentales.
item.dc:creator: Cecilia Kang, Ryan Mac and Eli Tan

media:content.media:credit: Mark Abramson para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: El presidente y director ejecutivo de Meta, Mark Zuckerberg, en el Tribunal Superior de Los Ángeles el mes pasado.

item.title: Trump Draws Bipartisan Backlash for Easing Oil Sanctions on Russia and Iran
item.description: Republicans and Democrats alike have criticized the Trump administration’s moves, taken to stabilize oil markets rocked by the war with Iran, warning that it is benefiting two U.S. adversaries.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Amit Dave/Reuters
media:content.media:description: A tanker carrying Russian oil in Gujarat, India, earlier this month. The Trump administration has defended the lifting of restrictions on Russia and Iran as necessary to prevent further spikes in energy prices that could ripple through the global economy and hit American consumers.

item.title: U.S.P.S. Plans to Impose 8% Surcharge to Offset Rising Transportation Costs Amid Iran War
item.description: The temporary price increase, which would take effect on April 26, comes amid a spike in fuel prices tied to the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Madeleine Ngo
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Rachel Wisniewski/Reuters
media:content.media:description: A post office in Philadelphia. The agency delivers to roughly 168 million addresses, covering every state, city and town.

item.title: Day Care Worker Stole Millions for Trips and W.W.E. Tickets, U.S. Says
item.description: Murielle Misczak was arrested Wednesday morning and is accused of using the money on wrestling tickets, luxury vacations and food deliveries.
item.dc:creator: Santul Nerkar

media:content.media:credit: Benjamin Norman for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A former day care director pleaded not guilty on Wednesday in Brooklyn federal court to charges she stole millions of dollars from her former employer.

item.title: Canada’s Supreme Court Hears Case on Ability to Suspend Constitutional Rights
item.description: Quebec’s ban on religious symbols — and a measure that suspends constitutional rights — are being tested in a case with far-reaching repercussions.
item.dc:creator: Norimitsu Onishi

media:content.media:credit: Amber Bracken/Reuters
media:content.media:description: The Supreme Court of Canada in Ottawa has devoted four days this week to hearings on the notwithstanding clause, which allows governments to pass laws that suspend rights in the Constitution’s Charter of Rights and Freedoms.

item.title: Leon Radvinsky, 43, Dies; Built the Adult-Entertainment Giant OnlyFans
item.description: By leveraging social media and the influencer economy, he turned his website into a byword for online pornography in the 21st century.
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Wednesday
item.description: The fighting continued while conditions for a possible cease-fire were debated.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A damaged home in an apartment building in Bnei Brak, Israel, on Wednesday.
